title: Allowance
description:
As a reward for record milk production, Farmer John has decided to start paying Bessie a small weekly allowance.

FJ has a set of coins in N (1 <= N <= 20) different denominations, where each denomination of coin evenly divides the next-larger denomination.

Using the given set of coins, he would like to pay Bessie at least some given amount of money C (1 <= C <= 100,000,000) every week. Please help him compute the maximum number of weeks he can pay Bessie.
input:
• Line 1: Two space-separated integers: N and C
  • Lines 2..N+1: Each line corresponds to a denomination of coin and contains two integers: the value V (1 <= V <= 100,000,000) of the denomination, and the number of coins B (1 <= B <= 1,000,000) of this denomation in Farmer John's possession.
output: • Line 1: A single integer that is the number of weeks Farmer John can pay Bessie at least C allowance
hint: FJ can overpay Bessie with the one 10-cent coin for 1 week, then pay Bessie two 5-cent coins for 10 weeks and then pay Bessie one 1-cent coin and one 5-cent coin for 100 weeks.
source: Olympiad, USA Computing Olympiad, 2009-2010 Season, USACO October 2009 Contest, Gold, Olympiad, USA Computing Olympiad, 2005-2006 Season, USACO October 2005 Contest, Silver
